channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Valentine’s Day scams to watch for this week
item.description: As Valentine's Day approaches, scammers are looking to deceive people. Here are the top scams to be aware of, and how to avoid falling for them.
item.dc:creator: Zach Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Dog seen riding in unsecured kennel along Indiana expressway now recovering
item.description: Video showed the dog in a kennel on a trailer that was being towed along an Indiana expressway in frigid temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Duolingo owl dead, killed by Cybertruck, company says
item.description: (KRON) -- In what some are calling a "master class" in killing off a company mascot, language-learning app Duolingo announced Tuesday that its trademark mascot, "Duo," aka "The Duolingo Owl," is dead.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Egg prices hit a record high as the U.S. contends with an ongoing bird flu outbreak, but consumers didn't need government figures released Wednesday to tell them eggs are terribly expensive and hard to find at times because of an ongoing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Eastern storm cuts power to tens of thousands as California braces for flooding
item.description: Storms dumped heavy snow and freezing rain on a swath of the eastern U.S. stretching from Kentucky to the nation's capital, causing hundreds of traffic accidents, knocking out power in places and threatening to flood waterways as temperatures began rising Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belarus releases 3 people, including an American and a jailed journalist
item.description: U.S. officials say Belarus has released three people, including an American and a journalist, who’d been detained for more than three years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Your closed Party City may soon turn into one of these familiar stores
item.description: What used to be your local Party City store may soon be selling books, recliner chairs, cowboy boots or discount merchandise.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KREX 5

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As DOGE hammers away at the US government, Republicans stir with quiet objections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Katie Britt has been working to make sure the Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency doesn't hit what she called “life-saving, groundbreaking research at high-achieving institutions,” including her state's beloved University of Alabama.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: When does a heartbeat start? South Carolina Supreme Court again takes up abortion issue
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — With a heartbeat abortion ban solidly in place in South Carolina, lawyers for the state and Planned Parenthood return to the state's highest court Wednesday to argue how restrictive the ban should be.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: School gym roof collapses under 350 tons of snow
item.description: A recent snowstorm that pummeled parts of Oregon caused the roof at a high school gym to collapse under the weight of about 700,000 pounds of snow, school officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California city passes controversial homeless camping ban
item.description: The ordinance, which allows the city to fine those who help the homeless and makes it illegal for homeless people to camp on public property, was approved in a 6-1 vote after more than two hours of public comments.
item.dc:creator: Joey Horta, Fox 5

item.title: Woman linked to reporter's death faced similar accusations in Las Vegas
item.description: The woman arrested in connection with a sports reporter’s death outside New Orleans has an active warrant in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Portable chargers to keep your phone powered anywhere
item.description: No one likes a dying phone battery, but thankfully, you’ve got options. Here are some of the latest gadgets to keep you powered up! UAG Rugged Wireless Power Bank and Stand, $100 This 10,000mAh battery can charge your phone at least twice.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Republican lawmakers urge Trump to respect federal court rulings
item.description: GOP lawmakers are growing increasingly worried about the nation plunging into a constitutional crisis if Trump ignores court orders halting his most aggressive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Coast Guard intercepts 132 Haitians on boat south of Florida Keys, sends them back home
item.description: Coast Guard cutter Escanaba stopped the 30-foot vessel overloaded with illegal aliens from Haiti approximately 50 miles southeast of Marathon, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US special envoy says another American hostage set to return home
item.description: Just when one American hostage detained in Russia gets ready to return to the U.S. on Tuesday, officials said another hostage is set to be released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court sets March 19 date for first execution in Arizona in over 2 years
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Arizona Supreme Court set a March 19 execution date Tuesday for a man who pleaded guilty to murder more than 17 years ago and recently said his death sentence was “long overdue.” It would be the state’s first application of the death penalty in more than two years.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Flu activity now highest in 15 years: See which states have it worst
item.description: Influenza cases continue to mount with the virus reaching activity levels in the U.S. not seen for the last 15 years, according to the Centers from Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In rare case, Georgia father jailed after his son was accused of a school shooting is granted bond
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — In a rare case of a parent being charged after a school shooting, a judge on Tuesday granted a $500,000 bond for the father of a 14-year-old boy accused of a deadly attack at a north Georgia high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Daily average of known gotaways at southern border plummets, down 93% from Biden administration highs
item.description: The average daily number of known gotaways at the southern border has dropped 93% since President Donald Trump returned to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With public broadcasters pressured by the Trump administration, PBS shuts down its diversity office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — PBS says it is shutting down its office of diversity, equity and inclusion to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order, firing the two executives brought on when the effort was begun in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Musk joins Trump in Oval Office to tout DOGE
item.description: Despite Musk’s pledge to be “maximally transparent,” the White House on Tuesday fired the inspector general for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tren de Aragua crackdown: 8 Venezuelan gang members indicted for sex trafficking
item.description: The U.S. Attorney's Office and law enforcement agencies in Tennessee announced the indictment of multiple people in the state with ties to the violent Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia residents outsmarted home invaders, stole their getaway car: prosecutor
item.description: Dogs really are a man's best friend – In Norfolk, Virginia one even provided a distraction that allowed a home invasion victim to escape.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Salman Rushdie testifies about his shock and pain as an attacker repeatedly stabbed him on stage
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — With a mix of humor and graphic detail, Salman Rushdie calmly told a jury Tuesday about the frenzied moments in August 2022 when a masked man rushed at him on a stage in western New York and repeatedly slashed him with a knife, leaving him with terrible injuries.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Detroit police say 2 kids died in a cold car after months without a permanent home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Two children who likely froze to death in a van outside a Detroit casino had been living in the vehicle with their family for a few months, moving around when possible, authorities said Tuesday. Their mother had contacted City Hall on Nov.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: How photos lost in American disasters find their way home, with a little help from people who care
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hollowed-out homes. Cars entombed by mud. Unpeopled roads. Belongings reduced to dirt and debris. It all took a toll on Taylor Schenker. After Hurricane Helene last September, Schenker was upset by the deluge of images of Asheville, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: ANNA FURMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Recent aviation disasters cause fears about the safety of flying
item.description: The spate of recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington, the fiery plane crash in Philadelphia and a plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 are only the most high-profile disasters.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the possibility of Trump serving a third term
item.description: Murmurs from President Trump and his allies have stirred up questions of the incumbent possibly seeking a third term in office, even as he’s currently constitutionally barred from doing so.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Judge directs Trump administration to comply with order to unfreeze federal grants
item.description: A federal judge in Rhode Island ordered the Trump administration to comply with his order to unfreeze federal grants, after attorneys general for several Democratic states claimed the directive was not being fulfilled. In a short order Monday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Stage and film actor Tony Roberts dies at 85
item.description: Tony Roberts, a versatile, Tony Award-nominated theater performer at home in both plays and musicals and who appeared in several Woody Allen movies — often as Allen's best friend — has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Efforts to put carbon dioxide underground face less support in Trump's second term
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Putting carbon dioxide from power plants and industrial facilities underground where it won’t contribute to global warming could see less federal support and enthusiasm under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, ABC 4
